Source: bemap-maplibre/bemap-tiles-auth.js

/**
 * BeNomad BeMap JavaScript API — TilesAuth (v2.0)
 *
 * JWT lifecycle for the BeNomad Tiles Worker. Replaces the buggy
 * global `window.fetch` patch that the v1 prototype shipped with.
 *
 * Responsibilities:
 *  - POST credentials to `Context.getTilesLoginUrl()` and store the JWT.
 *  - Parse the `exp` claim and schedule **proactive** renewal 5 min before
 *    expiry; fall back to 55 min when `exp` is unparseable.
 *  - Renew **reactively** on `401` errors caught from MapLibre's `error`
 *    event, then trigger a `setStyle()` reload to retry pending tiles.
 *  - On `document.visibilitychange` → `visible`: full re-login if the token
 *    is already expired, proactive refresh if within 5 min, no-op otherwise.
 *  - On `navigator.onLine === false`: queue the renewal until `online`.
 *  - Expose `attachTransformRequest(nativeMap)` that injects the
 *    `X-Session-Token` header on every request whose origin matches the
 *    configured `tilesHost`. The closure captures `getToken()` not the
 *    token value, so renewal is transparent.
 *  - Keep the last 3 tokens in `_history` for diagnostics.
 *
 * Tokens are stored according to `Context.tokenStorage`:
 *  - `'session'` — `sessionStorage` (default; cleared on tab close)
 *  - `'local'`   — `localStorage` (persists across reloads)
 *  - `'memory'`  — never persisted
 *
 * @since 2.0.0
 * @public
 * @constructor
 * @param {bemap.Context} ctx
 * @param {Object} [options]
 * @param {Function} [options.onError] Called with a `bemap.Error` on auth / network failures.
 * @param {Function} [options.onToken] Called with `{ reason, token }` on token changes.
 */
bemap.TilesAuth = function (ctx, options) {
  var opts = options || {};
  this.ctx = ctx;
  this._token = null;
  this._exp = 0;
  this._history = [];
  this._renewalTimer = null;
  this._queuedRenewal = false;
  this._onError = typeof opts.onError === 'function' ? opts.onError : function () {};
  this._onToken = typeof opts.onToken === 'function' ? opts.onToken : function () {};

  this._storageKey = 'bemap_tiles_token';
  this._storage = this._resolveStorage(ctx && ctx.tokenStorage);
  this._restoreFromStorage();

  this._installLifecycleHooks();
};

bemap.TilesAuth.STORAGE_KEY = 'bemap_tiles_token';

/* ============================================================
 * Module-scope interceptor state — NOT enumerable on the
 * `bemap.TilesAuth` constructor so that same-origin scripts
 * cannot pre-poison the registry by writing to
 * `bemap.TilesAuth._registry['mptiles-api-beta.benomad.net'] = …`.
 * Test code reaches in via the accessor helpers below.
 * ============================================================ */
var _tilesAuthState = {
  registry: {},           // { tilesHost → auth }
  patched: false,
  originalFetch: null,
  fastHostList: []        // for cheap substring short-circuit in the wrapper
};

// Accessors retained for tests (they need to reset / inspect state).
bemap.TilesAuth._getRegistry = function () { return _tilesAuthState.registry; };
bemap.TilesAuth._resetForTest = function () {
  _tilesAuthState.registry = {};
  _tilesAuthState.patched = false;
  _tilesAuthState.originalFetch = null;
  _tilesAuthState.fastHostList = [];
};
// Backwards-compatibility shims for the older test file — read-only.
Object.defineProperty(bemap.TilesAuth, '_registry',      { get: function () { return _tilesAuthState.registry; }, set: function (v) { _tilesAuthState.registry = v || {}; } });
Object.defineProperty(bemap.TilesAuth, '_fetchPatched',  { get: function () { return _tilesAuthState.patched;  }, set: function (v) { _tilesAuthState.patched = !!v; } });
Object.defineProperty(bemap.TilesAuth, '_originalFetch', { get: function () { return _tilesAuthState.originalFetch; }, set: function (v) { _tilesAuthState.originalFetch = v; } });

/**
 * Install a scoped `window.fetch` wrapper that injects `X-Session-Token`
 * on every request whose host matches `tilesHost`. The wrapper awaits
 * the in-flight login when the token is not yet available, so the very
 * first PMTiles range request fired by `pmtiles.js` (which bypasses
 * MapLibre's `transformRequest`) transparently waits for the JWT.
 *
 * Idempotent — calling it twice on the same page is a no-op. Multi-host
 * scenarios accumulate by registering the second auth's host into a
 * shared map.
 *
 * @public
 * @since 2.0.0
 * @param {bemap.TilesAuth} auth
 * @param {String} tilesHost
 */
bemap.TilesAuth.installFetchInterceptor = function (auth, tilesHost) {
  if (!auth || !tilesHost || typeof window === 'undefined' || typeof window.fetch !== 'function') return;
  _tilesAuthState.registry[tilesHost] = auth;
  if (_tilesAuthState.fastHostList.indexOf(tilesHost) === -1) _tilesAuthState.fastHostList.push(tilesHost);
  if (_tilesAuthState.patched) return;
  _tilesAuthState.patched = true;
  _tilesAuthState.originalFetch = window.fetch.bind(window);
  var orig = function (input, init) { return _tilesAuthState.originalFetch(input, init); };
  window.fetch = function (input, init) {
    var url = (typeof input === 'string') ? input : (input && input.url) || '';
    if (!url) return orig(input, init);
    // Cheap substring short-circuit — avoids the cost of `new URL()`
    // parsing on the dozens of unrelated fetches a typical page issues.
    var fast = false;
    for (var i = 0; i < _tilesAuthState.fastHostList.length; i++) {
      if (url.indexOf(_tilesAuthState.fastHostList[i]) !== -1) { fast = true; break; }
    }
    if (!fast) return orig(input, init);
    // Now confirm with strict host parsing (substring would match
    // `https://evil.com/?h=mptiles-api-beta.benomad.net`).
    var host = null;
    try { host = new URL(url, window.location.href).host; } catch (e) { /* malformed */ }
    if (!host) return orig(input, init);
    var registered = _tilesAuthState.registry[host];
    if (!registered) return orig(input, init);
    // Login itself uses Basic Auth — never inject the JWT into /api/login.
    if (url.indexOf('/api/login') !== -1) return orig(input, init);
    // For every other Worker URL, await the token then inject the header.
    return registered.whenTokenReady().then(function (token) {
      var newInit = Object.assign({}, init || {});
      var headers = new Headers((init && init.headers) || (input && input.headers) || {});
      if (token && !headers.has('X-Session-Token')) headers.set('X-Session-Token', token);
      newInit.headers = headers;
      return orig(input, newInit);
    }, function () {
      // Login failed — let the request fly without the header so the
      // server's 401 surfaces through the normal error channels rather
      // than this wrapper swallowing it.
      return orig(input, init);
    });
  };
};

/**
 * Uninstall the per-host registration. When the registry becomes empty,
 * restore the original `window.fetch` so SPA navigation does not leak
 * the patch across map lifecycles.
 * @public
 * @since 2.0.0
 */
bemap.TilesAuth.uninstallFetchInterceptor = function (tilesHost) {
  if (!tilesHost) return;
  delete _tilesAuthState.registry[tilesHost];
  var idx = _tilesAuthState.fastHostList.indexOf(tilesHost);
  if (idx !== -1) _tilesAuthState.fastHostList.splice(idx, 1);
  var empty = true;
  for (var k in _tilesAuthState.registry) { if (Object.prototype.hasOwnProperty.call(_tilesAuthState.registry, k)) { empty = false; break; } }
  if (empty && _tilesAuthState.patched && _tilesAuthState.originalFetch) {
    window.fetch = _tilesAuthState.originalFetch;
    _tilesAuthState.patched = false;
    _tilesAuthState.originalFetch = null;
  }
};

bemap.TilesAuth.prototype._resolveStorage = function (mode) {
  if (mode === 'memory') return null;
  try {
    if (mode === 'local' && typeof localStorage !== 'undefined') return localStorage;
    if (typeof sessionStorage !== 'undefined') return sessionStorage;
  } catch (e) { /* private mode / disabled storage */ }
  return null;
};

bemap.TilesAuth.prototype._restoreFromStorage = function () {
  if (!this._storage) return;
  try {
    var raw = this._storage.getItem(this._storageKey);
    if (!raw) return;
    var parsed = JSON.parse(raw);
    if (parsed && parsed.token) {
      this._token = parsed.token;
      this._exp = parsed.exp || 0;
      this._scheduleRenewal();
    }
  } catch (e) { /* ignore corrupt storage */ }
};

bemap.TilesAuth.prototype._persist = function () {
  if (!this._storage) return;
  try {
    this._storage.setItem(this._storageKey, JSON.stringify({ token: this._token, exp: this._exp }));
  } catch (e) { /* quota / disabled */ }
};

bemap.TilesAuth.prototype._clearStorage = function () {
  if (!this._storage) return;
  try { this._storage.removeItem(this._storageKey); } catch (e) { /* ignore */ }
};

/**
 * Parse the `exp` claim from a JWT, return its value in seconds since epoch,
 * or `0` when the token cannot be parsed.
 * @private
 */
bemap.TilesAuth.prototype._parseExp = function (token) {
  if (!token || typeof token !== 'string') return 0;
  var parts = token.split('.');
  if (parts.length < 2) return 0;
  try {
    var payload = parts[1].replace(/-/g, '+').replace(/_/g, '/');
    while (payload.length % 4) payload += '=';
    var json = (typeof atob === 'function')
      ? atob(payload)
      : Buffer.from(payload, 'base64').toString('utf8');
    var claims = JSON.parse(json);
    return typeof claims.exp === 'number' ? claims.exp : 0;
  } catch (e) { return 0; }
};

/**
 * POST credentials and persist the new JWT.
 * @public
 * @param {String} [login]    Defaults to `ctx.login`.
 * @param {String} [password] Defaults to `ctx.password`.
 * @return {Promise<String>} The new JWT.
 */
bemap.TilesAuth.prototype.login = function (login, password) {
  var self = this;
  // Track the in-flight login promise so whenTokenReady() can await it
  // without starting a second parallel login on every tile request.
  if (this._loginInFlight) return this._loginInFlight;
  var url = this.ctx && this.ctx.getTilesLoginUrl();
  if (!url) {
    return Promise.reject(new bemap.Error({
      code: bemap.Error.UNAUTHORIZED,
      message: 'TilesAuth.login: no tilesHost configured on Context'
    }));
  }
  var user = login || (this.ctx && this.ctx.login);
  var pass = password || (this.ctx && this.ctx.password);
  if (!user || !pass) {
    // Loud, one-shot warning so the developer sees the cause immediately
    // in the DevTools console (instead of just an unexplained 401 on the
    // first PMTiles range request).
    if (!bemap.TilesAuth._missingCredsWarned) {
      bemap.TilesAuth._missingCredsWarned = true;
      if (typeof console !== 'undefined' && console.warn) {
        console.warn(
          '[bemap.TilesAuth] No credentials on the Context — BeNomad Tiles\n' +
          '   requests will all 401 because no /api/login POST can be sent.\n' +
          '   Set `login` and `password` on your `new bemap.Context({...})`,\n' +
          '   OR remove `tilesHost` to fall back to WMS.'
        );
      }
    }
    return Promise.reject(new bemap.Error({
      code: bemap.Error.UNAUTHORIZED,
      message: 'TilesAuth.login: missing credentials on Context (set ctx.login + ctx.password, or remove ctx.tilesHost)'
    }));
  }
  var basic = 'Basic ' + ((typeof btoa === 'function')
    ? btoa(user + ':' + pass)
    : Buffer.from(user + ':' + pass).toString('base64'));

  if (typeof navigator !== 'undefined' && navigator.onLine === false) {
    self._queuedRenewal = true;
    var err = new bemap.Error({ code: bemap.Error.OFFLINE, message: 'TilesAuth.login: offline, queued' });
    self._onError(err);
    return Promise.reject(err);
  }

  var promise = fetch(url, {
    method: 'POST',
    headers: { 'Authorization': basic, 'Accept': 'application/json' }
  }).then(function (resp) {
    if (!resp.ok) {
      var code = resp.status === 401 ? bemap.Error.UNAUTHORIZED
        : resp.status === 403 ? bemap.Error.FORBIDDEN
        : resp.status === 429 ? bemap.Error.RATE_LIMITED
        : bemap.Error.NETWORK;
      var e = new bemap.Error({ code: code, status: resp.status, url: url, message: 'TilesAuth.login failed' });
      self._onError(e);
      throw e;
    }
    return resp.json().catch(function () { return null; });
  }).then(function (body) {
    var token = (body && (body.token || body.jwt || body.accessToken)) || null;
    if (!token) {
      var e = new bemap.Error({ code: bemap.Error.UNAUTHORIZED, message: 'TilesAuth.login: response had no token' });
      self._onError(e);
      throw e;
    }
    if (self._token) {
      // History records only the exp claim, NEVER the full JWT — see
      // SECURITY review. Subscribers that log `auth.getHistory()` must
      // not be able to exfiltrate a still-valid expired token.
      self._history.unshift({ exp: self._exp });
      if (self._history.length > 3) self._history.length = 3;
    }
    self._token = token;
    self._exp = self._parseExp(token);
    self._persist();
    self._scheduleRenewal();
    // Only emit `'refresh'` notifications from refresh(); skip the
    // synthetic `'login'` event during a refresh cycle so subscribers
    // see exactly one event per logical operation.
    if (!self._inRefresh) {
      self._onToken({ reason: 'login', exp: self._exp });
    }
    return token;
  });
  // Track and clear on settle. Both success and failure clear, so a
  // failed login does not pin a poisoned promise on subsequent calls.
  this._loginInFlight = promise;
  promise.then(
    function () { self._loginInFlight = null; },
    function () { self._loginInFlight = null; }
  );
  return promise;
};

/**
 * Resolve with the current valid JWT, kicking off (or joining) a login
 * if no token is available. The synchronisation primitive that the
 * fetch interceptor uses to delay PMTiles range requests until login
 * completes — without making the MapLibreMap constructor itself async.
 *
 * @public
 * @since 2.0.0
 * @return {Promise<String|null>}
 */
bemap.TilesAuth.prototype.whenTokenReady = function () {
  if (this._token && this.isValid()) return Promise.resolve(this._token);
  if (this._loginInFlight) return this._loginInFlight;
  return this.login();
};

/** @return {String|null} */
bemap.TilesAuth.prototype.getToken = function () { return this._token; };

/** @return {Boolean} */
bemap.TilesAuth.prototype.isValid = function () {
  if (!this._token) return false;
  if (!this._exp) return true; // unparseable exp — assume valid until 401
  var nowSec = Date.now() / 1000;
  return this._exp > nowSec + 30;
};

/**
 * Returns the last-3 history of superseded tokens — only the `exp` claim,
 * never the raw JWT.
 * @return {Array<{exp:Number}>}
 */
bemap.TilesAuth.prototype.getHistory = function () { return this._history.slice(); };

/**
 * Proactive renewal — re-uses the original credentials. Returns the new
 * token. Subscribers see exactly one `_onToken({ reason: 'refresh' })`
 * event per logical refresh; the synthetic `'login'` event is suppressed
 * during refresh to avoid the 3-events-per-cycle problem.
 *
 * @public
 * @return {Promise<String>}
 */
bemap.TilesAuth.prototype.refresh = function () {
  if (this._refreshInFlight) return this._refreshInFlight;
  var self = this;
  this._inRefresh = true;
  var p = this.login().then(function (t) {
    self._inRefresh = false;
    self._refreshInFlight = null;
    self._onToken({ reason: 'refresh', exp: self._exp });
    return t;
  }, function (err) {
    self._inRefresh = false;
    self._refreshInFlight = null;
    throw err;
  });
  this._refreshInFlight = p;
  return p;
};

bemap.TilesAuth.prototype._scheduleRenewal = function () {
  if (this._renewalTimer) {
    clearTimeout(this._renewalTimer);
    this._renewalTimer = null;
  }
  var ms;
  if (this._exp) {
    ms = (this._exp * 1000) - Date.now() - 5 * 60 * 1000;
  } else {
    ms = 55 * 60 * 1000;
  }
  ms = Math.max(0, Math.min(24 * 60 * 60 * 1000, ms));
  var self = this;
  this._renewalTimer = setTimeout(function () {
    self.refresh().catch(function () { /* error already surfaced */ });
  }, ms);
};

bemap.TilesAuth.prototype._installLifecycleHooks = function () {
  // Idempotency guard — re-entrant constructors / subclass init must
  // not stack duplicate listeners. `destroy()` removes whatever set
  // here, but only the first set; a second install would leak.
  if (this._lifecycleInstalled) return;
  this._lifecycleInstalled = true;
  var self = this;
  if (typeof document !== 'undefined' && typeof document.addEventListener === 'function') {
    this._visibilityHandler = function () {
      if (document.visibilityState !== 'visible') return;
      if (!self._token) return;
      if (!self.isValid()) {
        self.refresh().catch(function () { /* surfaced via _onError */ });
      } else if (self._exp && (self._exp - Date.now() / 1000) < 5 * 60) {
        self.refresh().catch(function () { /* surfaced */ });
      }
    };
    document.addEventListener('visibilitychange', this._visibilityHandler);
  }
  if (typeof window !== 'undefined' && typeof window.addEventListener === 'function') {
    this._onlineHandler = function () {
      if (self._queuedRenewal) {
        self._queuedRenewal = false;
        self.refresh().catch(function () { /* surfaced */ });
      }
    };
    window.addEventListener('online', this._onlineHandler);
  }
};

/**
 * Build the MapLibre `transformRequest` function. Injects
 * `X-Session-Token` on every request whose origin matches `ctx.tilesHost`.
 * The closure captures `this.getToken` so token renewal is transparent.
 *
 * Surface the result as `map.native = new maplibregl.Map({ transformRequest: auth.buildTransformRequest() })`.
 *
 * @public
 * @return {Function}
 */
bemap.TilesAuth.prototype.buildTransformRequest = function () {
  var self = this;
  var host = this.ctx && this.ctx.tilesHost;
  return function (url, resourceType) {
    if (!host) return { url: url };
    var matches = false;
    try {
      var parsed = new URL(url, (typeof window !== 'undefined' && window.location && window.location.href) || 'http://localhost/');
      matches = (parsed.host === host);
    } catch (e) {
      matches = (url.indexOf('://' + host) > -1) || (url.indexOf('//' + host) === 0);
    }
    if (!matches) return { url: url };
    var token = self.getToken();
    var headers = {};
    if (token) headers['X-Session-Token'] = token;
    return { url: url, headers: headers };
  };
};

/**
 * Mark a tile / style request as failed with HTTP 401 — triggers a refresh
 * followed by a `setStyle` reload of the current style spec, so the renewed
 * token is picked up by every pending request.
 *
 * @public
 * @param {Object} nativeMap MapLibre `map.native` instance.
 * @param {Object} [currentStyleSpec] Style currently displayed, for the reload.
 * @return {Promise<String>}
 */
bemap.TilesAuth.prototype.handle401 = function (nativeMap, currentStyleSpec) {
  // N simultaneous 401 errors → one shared refresh + one shared
  // setStyle reload. Without this dedup, a 401-storm produces N
  // parallel `setStyle` calls and N overlay catalog replays.
  if (this._handle401InFlight) return this._handle401InFlight;
  var self = this;
  var p = this.refresh().then(function (token) {
    if (nativeMap && currentStyleSpec && typeof nativeMap.setStyle === 'function') {
      try { nativeMap.setStyle(currentStyleSpec); } catch (e) { /* ignore */ }
    }
    self._handle401InFlight = null;
    return token;
  }, function (err) {
    self._handle401InFlight = null;
    throw err;
  });
  this._handle401InFlight = p;
  return p;
};

/**
 * Clear the token, the history, the storage, and the renewal timer.
 * Optionally POST to the Worker logout endpoint to revoke server-side.
 *
 * @public
 * @param {Object} [options]
 * @param {Boolean} [options.serverSide=false] When true, fire-and-forget POST `/api/logout`.
 * @return {Promise<void>}
 */
bemap.TilesAuth.prototype.logout = function (options) {
  var opts = options || {};
  var token = this._token;
  this._token = null;
  this._exp = 0;
  this._history.length = 0;
  this._clearStorage();
  if (this._renewalTimer) {
    clearTimeout(this._renewalTimer);
    this._renewalTimer = null;
  }
  if (opts.serverSide && this.ctx && typeof fetch === 'function') {
    var url = this.ctx.getTilesLogoutUrl();
    if (url && token) {
      return fetch(url, { method: 'POST', headers: { 'X-Session-Token': token } })
        .then(function () {})
        .catch(function () {});
    }
  }
  return Promise.resolve();
};

/**
 * Detach lifecycle listeners. Call from `MapLibreMap.remove()`.
 * @public
 */
bemap.TilesAuth.prototype.destroy = function () {
  this._destroyed = true;
  if (this._renewalTimer) {
    clearTimeout(this._renewalTimer);
    this._renewalTimer = null;
  }
  if (typeof document !== 'undefined' && this._visibilityHandler) {
    document.removeEventListener('visibilitychange', this._visibilityHandler);
    this._visibilityHandler = null;
  }
  if (typeof window !== 'undefined' && this._onlineHandler) {
    window.removeEventListener('online', this._onlineHandler);
    this._onlineHandler = null;
  }
  this._lifecycleInstalled = false;
  // Remove our entry from the interceptor's registry — if we were the
  // last entry, the wrapper restores `window.fetch` to its original.
  var host = this.ctx && this.ctx.tilesHost;
  if (host && typeof bemap.TilesAuth.uninstallFetchInterceptor === 'function') {
    bemap.TilesAuth.uninstallFetchInterceptor(host);
  }
};

Documentation generated by JSDoc 3.6.3 on Tue Jun 09 2026 18:17:43 GMT+0200 (heure d’été d’Europe centrale)
